questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Gross National Product (GNP) and National Income (NI) are the same.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The concept of GNP and NI is the same, but GNP is measured at market price while NI is measured at factor cost.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: GNP is measured at market price, which includes indirect taxes and excludes subsidies, whereas NI is measured at factor cost, which excludes indirect taxes and includes subsidies.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Gross Domestic Product (GDP) at factor cost is lower than GDP at market price.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Taxes are included in GDP at market price and not in GDP at factor cost.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: GDP at market price includes indirect taxes, whereas GDP at factor cost excludes indirect taxes.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The concept of Net Domestic Product (NDP) and Net National Product (NNP) is the same.
questions.reason: Reason (R): NDP and NNP both exclude depreciation and include only that part of depreciation which is not subtracted.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: NDP and NNP are indeed the same, as they both exclude depreciation and include only that part of depreciation which is not subtracted.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Disposable Income is the same as Personal Income.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Personal Income is the income received by an individual, whereas Disposable Income is the amount left after paying taxes.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Personal Income is the income received by an individual, including taxes, whereas Disposable Income is the amount left after paying taxes.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The concept of Gross National Disposable Income is the same as Gross National Income.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Gross National Disposable Income is the amount left after paying taxes and receiving transfers, whereas Gross National Income is the total income earned by a nation.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Gross National Disposable Income is not the same as Gross National Income, as the former excludes transfers.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Net National Income is the same as Net National Disposable Income.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Net National Income is the income earned by a nation after subtracting depreciation, whereas Net National Disposable Income is the amount left after paying taxes and receiving transfers.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Net National Income is the income earned by a nation after subtracting depreciation, whereas Net National Disposable Income is the amount left after paying taxes and receiving transfers.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The concept of National Income is the same as Personal Income.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Personal Income is the income received by an individual, whereas National Income is the total income earned by a nation.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Personal Income is the income received by an individual, whereas National Income is the total income earned by a nation.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Disposable Income is the same as Personal Disposable Income.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Personal Disposable Income is the amount left after paying taxes and receiving transfers, whereas Disposable Income is the amount left after paying taxes.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Personal Disposable Income is the amount left after paying taxes and receiving transfers, whereas Disposable Income is the amount left after paying taxes.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Gross National Disposable Income is higher than Gross National Income.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Gross National Disposable Income is the amount left after paying taxes and receiving transfers, which is always higher than Gross National Income.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Gross National Disposable Income is not necessarily higher than Gross National Income, as it depends on the transfers received.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Net National Disposable Income is the same as Net National Income.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Net National Disposable Income is the amount left after paying taxes and receiving transfers, whereas Net National Income is the income earned by a nation after subtracting depreciation.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
